
```{r}
df <- read.csv("glassdoor-companies-reviews.csv")

head(df)

```



```{r}
apply(is.na(df), 2, sum)

# Missing Check Function
Missing.Check.By.Column <- function(df){
  return (apply(is.na(df), 2, sum))
}

```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

df <- df %>% 
  drop_na(employee_length)

df$rating_year = as.numeric(sub("[^0-9]*(\\d{4}).*", "\\1", df$rating_date))

df[is.na(df$employee_type), "employee_type"] <- "Former"
# fill it as Former since they're all 
# todo  make it better: check whether endyear exists

df$employee_job_quit <- ifelse(grepl("^Former", df$employee_type, ignore.case = TRUE),TRUE, FALSE)

df$employee_job_start_year <- ifelse(
  df$employee_job_quit,
  df$employee_job_end_year - df$employee_length, # Former use the year quit
  df$rating_year - df$employee_length # Current: use the year of rating
)

lengthvars <- c("employee_job_start_year", "final_end_year", "employee_job_quit", "employee_length", "employee_type")
print(df[, lengthvars])

# is there anyone quit job without giving end year? no
# all missing of employee_job_end_year due to not quit job
df[df$employee_job_quit & is.na(df$employee_job_end_year), ]
```
